description: Renders a native <button> by default, or whatever element the consumer passes via asChild (through Radix Slot). All native button semantics apply: implicit role="button", disabled state with pointer-events:none and opacity-50. Variants and sizes only change the visual treatment.

keyboard.action: Moves focus to / from the button in document order.

focus.notes: Uses focus-visible:ring-1 so the ring only shows for keyboard focus, not mouse clicks. Disabled buttons drop pointer events and dim to 50% opacity.

announcements: Announces as "button" with the button's text content as its accessible name.
When disabled, announces the disabled state.
screenReader.notes: Icon-only buttons (size="icon") must include an accessible name via aria-label or a visually-hidden <span>; the icon alone is not enough.

contrast.text: Every variant meets WCAG AA against its background (primary, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link)
contrast.ui: Focus ring uses ring-ring which meets AA non-text contrast against bg-background

motion.reducedMotion: Only a color transition on hover; no motion-based animations

consumerResponsibilities: Provide an accessible name for icon-only buttons; the inner svg is marked pointer-events-none and carries no semantics.
Use type="submit" / type="reset" explicitly when inside a form; the component does not set it for you.
When using asChild, the child must itself be a focusable, activatable element (e.g. <a>, another <button>), otherwise keyboard activation breaks.
